name: Chinese Hot & Sour Soup
recipeIngredient: 6 Shiitake mushrooms - soaked in hot water for 20 minutes (For 20 minutes)
1/2 cup Canned bamboo shoots - cut into julienne strips
1 cup Tofu - diced into half-inch cubes
recipeInstructions:
1. In a hot soup, the chilies' fire is hidden in the Sichuan hot bean paste.
2. Heat the chicken stock to a boil. Add the chicken breasts, ginger root, and onion, and simmer for 35 minutes, covered. Remove the chicken, reserving the stock, and let cool. Remove the skin and bones, then shred the meat. Set aside.
3. Strain the chicken stock into a 4-quart pan and bring to a boil again.
4. Drain the soaking mushrooms, discard the stems, and thinly slice the caps. Add them to the stock with the soy sauce, rice wine, rice vinegar, hot bean paste, and white pepper. Reduce heat and simmer for 10 minutes.
5. Add the shredded chicken, bamboo shoots, and tofu, and simmer for another 2 minutes. Then bring to a full boil.
6. Beat the eggs well and, as you gently stir the soup, slowly add them in a thin stream.
7. Adjust seasoning for salt, hot bean paste, and tartness.
8. At serving time, add the green onion slices.
9. Makes 4 appetizer servings.
10. Per serving: 178 calories, 6 grams carbohydrates, 133 mg cholesterol, 348 mg sodium, 18 grams protein, 9 grams fat, 3 grams saturated fat.
